purpose: Non-secret Railway Postgres query metadata for Vault Provisioner-backed verification. No credentials.

connection_method.name: vault-provisioner-lease-psql
steps: Confirm Vault consumer is armed (GET /api/consumer/status via harness VAULT_URL).
register_run for folder=vygo and the mission run_id.
Issue a short-lived consumer lease for RAILWAY_TOKEN (provider=railway).
Use Railway GraphQL variables for project_id + environment_id + service_id Postgres-0MtT.
Connect with psql to DATABASE_PUBLIC_URL (not the .railway.internal DATABASE_URL).
Release the lease immediately after the SELECT.
Never write tokens, passwords, or secret-bearing connection strings into TESTLOG.md, verdicts, or commits.

approved_sql.success_exactly_one: SELECT id::text AS id, full_name, work_email, product_url, message, source, created_at::text AS created_at FROM applications WHERE full_name = 'Ratchet E2E Test' AND work_email = 'e2e-test+<run-marker>@vygo.ai';
approved_sql.success_count: SELECT count(*)::int AS n FROM applications WHERE full_name = 'Ratchet E2E Test' AND work_email = 'e2e-test+<run-marker>@vygo.ai';
approved_sql.invalid_zero: SELECT count(*)::int AS n FROM applications WHERE work_email = 'not-an-email';

harness_locations.run_shared_provision_summary: shared/provision_summary.json (non-secret; written by Vault Provisioner + builder enrichment)

secrets_policy: This document contains no tokens, passwords, or connection strings. Credentials come exclusively through the Vault Provisioner consumer lease path.
